memoryArtifacts.headline: Jean III de Brabant (1300-1355)
memoryArtifacts.description: Jean III de Brabant, né en 1300, mort à Bruxelles le 5 décembre 1355, est duc de Brabant et de Limbourg de 1312 à 1355. Il est le fils de Jean II, Duc de Brabant et de Limbourg, et de la Princesse Mar …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Hertog Jan III van Brabant於1299年10月20日出生在比利時福拉阿姆-布拉班特特倫時，他的父親Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT24歲，母親Margaret of England24歲。他與Ermengarde Elisabetha Maria Van Brant van Huldenberg在荷蘭布拉班特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他於1333年被列為hertog van brabant。他去世於1355年12月5日在荷蘭布拉班特布波瑟爾享年56歲，埋葬在比利時布拉班特 瓦隆維勒斯-拉-維爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hertog Jan III van Brabant于1299年10月20日出生在比利时福拉阿姆-布拉班特特倫时，他的父亲Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT24岁，母亲Margaret of England24岁。他与Ermengarde Elisabetha Maria Van Brant van Huldenberg在荷兰布拉班特结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他于1333年被列为hertog van brabant。他去世于1355年12月5日在荷兰布拉班特布波瑟爾享年56岁，埋葬在比利时布拉班特 瓦隆維勒斯-拉-維爾。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Brabant
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Aristide, Marcel, Normand, Ovila, Yvan.
French, Walloon, Flemish, and German: habitational name from Brabant, a province in the Low Countries.

English: cognate of 1 above, from Old French Brabant, Middle English Braban, Brabayn, used to denote both the region and an inhabitant of it. See also the synonymous Brabazon . In medieval England, Brabans were typically linen weavers and cloth merchants. The final syllable of Braban, when weakly stressed, was often altered to -ben, -bin, or -bon, and also to -bam. The additional -t or -d in post-medieval Brabant, Braband , etc. is probably excrescent, rather than a relic of the Old French form.

variants.name: Brabender
meanings: German (mainly Westphalia): habitational name for someone from Brabant (see   Brabant  ), perhaps applied also as a nickname for a merchant of Brabantine cloth.

variants.name: Brabazon
meanings: English: from Old French Brabançon, Anglo-Norman French Brabanzon, Middle English Brab(a)son ‘Brabanter’. This was originally an ethnic name for a native of the duchy of Brabant (now in north Belgium). Medieval Brabant was widely known for two exports, its brutal mercenary soldiers and its linen cloth. In the 13th century parts of France were devastated by marauding gangs of armed Brabanters, and brabançon came to be used generically to denote a mercenary, hired assassin, or robber. These originated in Brabant and Flanders, but in the course of time accepted recruits from anywhere. The true source of the surname is the presence in many English (and Irish) towns of Brabant cloth merchants.

variants.name: Bremner
meanings: Scottish: habitational name for someone from Brabant in the Low Countries, from Older Scots Brebner, Brabanare ‘native or inhabitant of Brabant’ (see Brabant and compare with the synonymous Brabazon ). Weavers from Brabant settled early at Aberdeen and on the east coast of Scotland.
German: habitational name for someone from the city of Bremen. This surname is very rare in Germany.

variants.name: Brabham
meanings: English: variant of Brabant , altered by association with English habitational names ending in -ham.

variants.name: Braband
meanings: German and English: variant of   Brabant  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: When Hertog Jan III van Brabant was born on 20 October 1299, in Tervuren, Flemish Brabant, Belgium, his father, Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT, was 24 and his mother, Margaret of England, was 24. He married Ermengarde Elisabetha Maria Van Brant van Huldenberg in Brabant, Netherlands. They were the parents of at least 1 son. In 1333, at the age of 34, his occupation is listed as hertog van brabant. He died on 5 December 1355, in Brussels, Brabant, Netherlands, at the age of 56, and was buried in Villers-la-Ville, Walloon Brabant, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hertog Jan III van Brabant est né le 20 octobre 1299, à Tervuren, Brabant Flamand, Belgique, son père, Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Margaret of England, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Ermengarde Elisabetha Maria Van Brant van Huldenberg à Brabant, Pays-Bas. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. En 1333, à l’âge de 34 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est hertog van brabant. Il est décédé le 5 décembre 1355, à Bruxelles, Brabant, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Villers-la-Ville, Brabant wallon, Belgique.

de.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlese: Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Hertog Jan III van Brabant am 20. Oktober 1299 in Tervuren, Flämisch Brabant, Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT 24 und seine Mutter Margaret of England 24. Er hat in Brabant, Niederlande Ermengarde Elisabetha Maria Van Brant van Huldenberg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. 1333, als er 34 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als hertog van brabant aufgeführt. Er starb am 5. Dezember 1355 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Brüssel, Brabant, Niederlande und wurde in Villers-la-Ville, Wallonisch Brabant, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hertog Jan III van Brabant è nato il il 20 ottobre 1299 nel Tervuren, Vlaams-Brabant, Belgio, il suo padre, Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Margaret of England, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Ermengarde Elisabetha Maria Van Brant van Huldenberg nel Brabant, Paesi Bassi. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Nel 1333, all'età di 34 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come hertog van brabant. Morì il 5 dicembre 1355, nel Brussel, Brabant, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolto a Villers-la-Ville, Brabant wallon, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Hertog Jan III van Brabantが1299年10月20日にベルギーvラアムス-ブラバントテルvウレンで生まれた時，父Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANTは24歳，母Margaret of Englandは24歳であった。オランダブラバントでErmengarde Elisabetha Maria Van Brant van Huldenbergと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。1333年，34歳の時，職業はhertog van brabantと記録されている。彼は1355年12月5日にオランダブラバントブルセルで56歳で死去し，ベルギーブラバント ワロンヴィラース-ラ-ヴィルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Hertog Jan III van Brabant(이)가 1299년 10월 20일 벨기에 블라아스-브라반트 터브런에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret of England(은)는 24세였다. 그는 네덜란드 브라반트에서 Ermengarde Elisabetha Maria Van Brant van Huldenberg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1333년, 34세의 나이로 그의 직업은 hertog van brabant이었다. 그는 1355년 12월 5일 네덜란드 브라반트 브루셀에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 브라반트 월런 비레르스-라-빌에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hertog Jan III van Brabant nasceu em 20 de outubro de 1299, em Tervuren, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, seu pai, Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Margaret of England, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Ermengarde Elisabetha Maria Van Brant van Huldenberg em Brabant, Países Baixos. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Em 1333, com 34 anos, sua profissão aparece como hertog van brabant. Ele faleceu em 5 de dezembro de 1355, em Bruxelas, Brabant, Países Baixos, com 56 anos, e foi sepultado em Villers-la-Ville, Brabant wallon, Bélgica.

ru.Westminster Abbey, Westminster, Middlese: вестминстир эбби, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hertog Jan III van Brabant родился 20 октября 1299 г. в местности тирвурен, влаамс-брабант, Бельгия, его отцу по имени Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret of England, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Ermengarde Elisabetha Maria Van Brant van Huldenberg в местности брабант, Нидерланды. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. В 1333 году, в возрасте 34 лет,  он работал по профессии hertog van brabant. Он умер 5 декабря 1355 г. в местности брассел, брабант, Нидерланды в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности виллирс-ла-вилл, брабант валлон, Бельгия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hertog Jan III van Brabant nació el 20 de octubre de 1299, en Tervuren, Vlaams-Brabant, Bélgica, su papá, Jean I I Le Pacifique De Vreedzame DE BRABANT, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Margaret of England, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Ermengarde Elisabetha Maria Van Brant van Huldenberg en Brabant, Países Bajos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. En 1333, a la edad de 34 años, su ocupación registrada era hertog van brabant. Murió el 5 de diciembre de 1355, en Brussel, Brabant, Países Bajos, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultado en Villers-la-Ville, Brabant wallon, Bélgica.
